programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: Bruno v Tyson
programme.desc: The story of the boxing rivalry between Frank Bruno and Mike Tyson, spanning two epic fights marred by politics and volatile race relations, with the old foes coming together for a reunion.

programme.title: Seen & Heard: The History of Black Television
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring African-American representation in Hollywood and TV, showcasing the creators and artists who shaped it. From Amos 'n' Andy to The Jeffersons and Good Times, black representation was historically shaped by white writers and creators, but in the 1990s, a rise in shows made by black people brought social issues to the forefront.

programme.title: Seen & Heard: The History of Black Television
programme.desc: Part two of two. Black creators discuss taking ownership of the narrative while highlighting the need for opportunities at every level of the industry.

programme.title: Barbie Uncovered: A Dreamhouse Divided
programme.desc: The story of the two people responsible for creating the Barbie doll - Ruth Handler, the Jewish immigrant who built the largest toy company in the world from nothing and designer Jack Ryan, the man said to have actually created the doll.

programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, who rose to fame through his roles in Romeo + Juliet and Titanic, and won a Best Actor Oscar for The Revenant.
